file_name: Yazdanparast et al. - 2024 - Comparison of the diagnostic accuracy of teledermoscopy, face-to-face examinations and artificial in.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The study used the Moleanalyzer Pro artificial intelligence (AI) Assistant, which is a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), to analyze dermoscopic images and classify melanocytic lesions as benign, malignant, or borderline for the diagnosis of melanoma.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is explicitly labeled as an 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' and describes an analytical cross-sectional study involving 29 patients and 30 melanocytic moles. It details specific methodology (teledermoscopy, face-to-face examination, AI assistant), statistical analysis, and original results comparing diagnostic accuracy against histopathology, which constitutes original experimental/observational research.

title: Comparison of the Diagnostic Accuracy of Teledermoscopy, Face‑to‑Face Examinations and Artificial Intelligence in the Diagnosis of Melanoma
brief_summary: This study compared the diagnostic accuracy of face-to-face clinical examinations, FotoFinder teledermoscopy, and the Moleanalyzer Pro artificial intelligence assistant for diagnosing melanoma against histopathology. The analytical cross-sectional study included 30 melanocytic moles from 29 patients at Tehran University of Medical Sciences. Face-to-face examination achieved the highest sensitivity (90.9%), while teledermoscopy demonstrated the highest specificity (78.9%) and agreement with the gold standard (73.33%). In contrast, the Moleanalyzer Pro AI assistant showed the lowest performance with a sensitivity of 36.4% and agreement of 40%. The authors concluded that teledermoscopy is valuable for reducing unnecessary biopsies, whereas the AI assistant is too inaccurate to replace dermatologist decision-making.

lesion_types_studied.details: Histopathological diagnosis of melanoma was confirmed in 5 out of 30 lesions. Classified as 'malignant' by the diagnostic methods.
Histopathological diagnosis of borderline lesions was confirmed in 6 out of 30 lesions. Classified as 'borderline' by the diagnostic methods. Atypical melanocytic moles were considered borderline.
Histopathological diagnosis of benign lesions was confirmed in 19 out of 30 lesions. Classified as 'benign' by the diagnostic methods. The study population consisted of 30 melanocytic moles.

all_task_types.target: Diagnostic category of melanocytic lesions (benign, malignant, or borderline)
all_task_types.details: The Moleanalyzer Pro AI assistant classifies dermoscopic images into three discrete diagnostic categories: benign (green), malignant (red), or borderline (yellow). The study evaluates the accuracy of this classification against histopathology gold standard.

datasets_used.name: Tehran University of Medical Sciences Clinical Cohort
datasets_used.description: A prospective, analytical cross-sectional study cohort comprising 30 melanocytic moles from 29 patients (age > 18) recruited from the Center for Research and Training in Skin Disease and Leprosy, Tehran University of Medical Sciences, between December 2019 and May 2022. The dataset includes dermoscopic images captured via FotoFinder device and face-to-face clinical assessments, with histopathology as the gold standard.

use.testing: The cohort was used to evaluate the diagnostic accuracy (sensitivity, specificity, agreement) of three methods: face-to-face examination, FotoFinder teledermoscopy, and Moleanalyzer Pro AI assistant against histopathology.
use.other: The Moleanalyzer Pro AI model used for analysis was pre-trained elsewhere (referenced as [10] in the text); this study served as an external clinical validation of that pre-existing model.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To evaluate inter-method reliability and agreement between the gold standard (histopathology) and the diagnostic methods (face-to-face, teledermoscopy, AI).
To assess the agreement between the diagnostic methods and the gold standard (histopathology).
To determine the diagnostic accuracy of the three methods for malignant and borderline versus benign lesions.
To determine statistical significance for the reported kappa coefficients.

hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: There is no agreement between the diagnostic method (face-to-face, teledermoscopy, or AI) and the gold standard (histopathology) beyond chance.
hypothesis_testing.alternative_hypothesis: There is significant agreement between the diagnostic method and the gold standard beyond chance.
hypothesis_testing.interpretation: Statistical significance was determined using a P-value threshold of < 0.05. The study reported P-values for the kappa coefficients of face-to-face examination (P > 0.001), teledermoscopy (P > 0.001), and the AI assistant (P = 0.53). The AI assistant's result was not statistically significant, while the other two methods showed significant agreement with the gold standard.

Tehran University of Medical Sciences Cl.notes: The study included 30 melanocytic moles from 29 patients. Histopathology confirmed 5 malignant, 6 borderline, and 19 benign lesions. Face-to-face examination had the highest sensitivity (90.9%). Teledermoscopy had the highest specificity (78.9%) and agreement (73.33%). Moleanalyzer Pro had the lowest performance with sensitivity 36.4% and agreement 40%. The AI assistant's agreement was not statistically significant (P=0.53).

architecture_used.main_model: Moleanalyzer Pro AI Assistant (Convolutional Neural Network)
architecture_used.pretraining: Not reported in this study; the paper states the architecture and training procedures are described in detail elsewhere (reference [10]).

architecture_details.manufacturer: FotoFinder Systems GmbH, Bad Birnbach Germany
architecture_details.approval_status: Market-approved medical device for the European market

training_details.details: Not reported in this study; details are cited in reference [10] (Haenssle HA, et al. Man against machine: Diagnostic performance of a deep learning convolutional neural network for dermoscopic melanoma recognition in comparison to 58 dermatologists. Ann Oncol 2018;29:1836‑42).

classification_or_output_head.output_type: Probability of the lesion being melanoma

preprocessing_techniques: Exclusion of lesions covered by thick hair
Exclusion of lesions with a history of previous biopsy or any type of treatment
Exclusion of lesions located on scars, psoriasis, eczema or other skin conditions
conclusions: Teledermoscopy using the FotoFinder device demonstrated the highest agreement with the reference histopathology diagnosis (73.33%) and the highest specificity (78.9%), which helps reduce unnecessary biopsy referrals.

The Moleanalyzer Pro artificial intelligence assistant showed the lowest performance with an agreement of only 40% and was found to be a poor technique compared to face-to-face examination and teledermoscopy.
The Moleanalyzer Pro AI assistant cannot replace dermatologist decision-making due to its low diagnostic accuracy.

The study acknowledges limitations including selection bias (only lesions requiring biopsy were included), a small sample size, and recruitment from a single center, which may affect the generalizability of the results.
Teledermoscopy is considered a valuable tool for the early diagnosis of melanoma and for screening, supporting the use of computer-aided diagnosis to assist dermatologists.
important_remarks: The study suffers from significant selection bias because it included only lesions that had already been referred for biopsy or excision, rather than a consecutive or unselected clinical population.
The sample size is very small (30 lesions from 29 patients), which limits the statistical power and generalizability of the results.
The study was conducted at a single center (Tehran University of Medical Sciences), which may affect the external validity of the findings.
Dermatologists were aware that only lesions requiring biopsy were included, which may have influenced their diagnostic decisions (observer bias).
The AI assistant (Moleanalyzer Pro) demonstrated poor performance with a Cohen’s kappa of -0.01 and a non-significant P-value of 0.53, indicating agreement no better than chance.
The AI assistant missed two malignant lesions (false negatives), highlighting a critical safety risk for its use in clinical decision-making.
The diagnostic accuracy of teledermoscopy was found to be dependent on the dermatologist's experience and access to patient history, which was unavailable in the teledermoscopy arm.
The Moleanalyzer Pro AI model used was pre-trained and validated in a separate study (Haenssle et al., 2018); this study served as an independent external validation which showed poor concordance.
